metadata.title: A Geometric Framework for Understanding Memorization in Generative Models
metadata.description: 该海报提出了一个基于局部内在维度（LID）的几何框架，用于理解和检测深度生成模型中的记忆现象，并将其分为过拟合驱动和数据驱动两种类型。

sections.title: Background
content: Memorization and Overfitting in Deep Generative Models
• Deep generative models are known to memorize and reproduce their training data
• This behaviour reduces model utility and may have severe privacy and legal implications
• Contributing factors [4]: data complexity, duplication of training points, and highly specific conditioning
• There is as yet no systematic framework describing memorization in DGMs
The Manifold Hypothesis
• High-dimensional data lies on a manifold $\mathcal{M}_*$ with multiple components of varying dimensionalities
• DGMs also generate samples from a manifold $\mathcal{M}_\theta$ with the goal of matching $\mathcal{M}_\theta$ to $\mathcal{M}_*$

• The local intrinsic dimension of $x \in \mathcal{M}_*$ (resp. $x \in \mathcal{M}_\theta$), denoted $\text{LID}_*(x)$ (resp. $\text{LID}_\theta(x)$), is the dimension of the manifold component containing $x$
• Intuitively, the LID of $x$ captures the complexity of the datapoint $x$ and constraints on the dataset's structure

sections.title: Our Geometric Framework
content: We propose that $x \in \mathcal{M}_*$ is memorized when $\text{LID}_\theta(x)$ is small.
• This encompasses both exact copying of training images ($\text{LID}_\theta(x) = 0$) and copying of styles or parts of an image as in Figure 2 ($\text{LID}_\theta(x) > 0$ but still small)
From this definition, memorization can be split into two types:
1. $\text{LID}_\theta(x) < \text{LID}_*(x)$: overfitting-driven memorization (OD-Mem), in which the model fails to generalize to the ground truth manifold correctly (Figure 1)

2. $\text{LID}_\theta(x) = \text{LID}_*(x)$: data-driven memorization (DD-Mem), in which the ground truth data distribution has been learned correctly, but itself contains very few degrees of freedom at $x$
Figure 1: LID values for three red model manifolds with different degrees of memorization of the three leftmost datapoints, which are depicted as dark blue crosses. The true manifold is light blue.

Figure 2: 8 images along a relatively low-dimensional manifold learned by Stable Diffusion v1.5. The first is a real image from LAION (flagged as memorized by [2]), and the remainder were generated by the model.

sections.title: Explaining Memorization
content: The geometric framework explains memorization-related phenomena in the literature:
Duplicated Data It has been observed that memorization occurs when training points are duplicated. Here we identify that data duplication is equivalent to an LID of 0:
Theorem (Informal). With high probability and a sufficiently large dataset, duplicate samples occur at $x_0$ if and only if $\text{LID}_*(x_0) = 0$.
Complexity. [4] observes that simple images are memorized more often. This is neatly explained by the fact that they have lower $\text{LID}_*$.
Conditioning. Highly-specific conditioning inputs encourage the reproduction of memorized samples. We explain this by the fact that conditioning on a prompt $c$ decreases LID:
Proposition. $\text{LID}_\theta(x|c) \leq \text{LID}_\theta(x)$.

Classifier-Free Guidance (CFG). A large classifier-free guidance vector in diffusion models correlates strongly with memorization [5]. This is explainable by observations in the literature that faster explosion of model norms during sampling corresponds to lower $\text{LID}_\theta$.

sections.title: Empirical Verification
content: We can test this framework using the $\text{LID}_\theta$ estimator of [3]:

von Mises Distribution We train a diffusion model on a von Mises distribution; in Figure 3b we depict model samples with their corresponding LIDs, and a couple of samples are clearly memorized with $\text{LID}_\theta(x) = 0$, while the remaining samples have $\text{LID}_\theta(x) = 1$.
Figure: Training a diffusion model on a von Mises distribution.
Stable Diffusion on LAION, COCO, and Tuxemon We compute $\text{LID}_\theta$ of Stable Diffusion [1] on
• Unmemorized images from a mix of LAION Aesthetics 6.5+, COCO, and Tuxemon
• Memorized images from LAION [2]
We find that low LIDs correspond better to memorized images than PNG size, a proxy for image complexity.
Figure 4: Density histograms for each memorization metric on the different datasets.
